channel.title: SEAtoday - Sports & Outdoors
channel.description: Upcoming events for SEAtoday - Sports & Outdoors

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

Event details

item.title: April Membership Meeting: King County Aquatic Center Update
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 11:45 AM

Location: Federal Way Performing Arts and Event Center, 31510 Pete von Reichbauer Way S, Federal Way, WA

Join us for a keynote from Meagan Viken, Facility Supervisor of the King County Aquatic Center. Meagan manages the King County Aquatic Center, where she focuses on both high-level competition and community access. Under her leadership, the facility has hosted major events such as NCAA Division I Nationals, US Master&'s Summer Nationals, Washington State Special Olympics, while also expanding public programming-adding over 700 swimming lessons in the past year alone.

Event details

item.title: Seattle Mariners vs. New York Yankees
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 1:10 PM

Location: T-Mobile Park, 1516 First Ave. S., Seattle, WA

Please visit our Ballpark Information Guide to make the most of your visit to T-Mobile Park. Find the latest information regarding Bag Policy, Prohibited Items, Transportation Options and other Frequently Asked Questions.

Event details

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

Event details

item.title: Seattle University Redhawks Baseball Vs Portland
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Bannerwood Park, 1790 Richards Rd, Bellevue, WA

Seattle University Baseball Vs. Portland on Thursday at 4:00PM Bannerwood Park: 1790 Richards Rd, Bellevue, WA 98005

Event details

item.title: LANDSCAPE TECHNICIAN BOOTCAMP
item.description:
Friday, April 3, 2026 at 7 AM

Location: Lake Washington Institute of Technology - LWTech, 11605 132nd Ave NE, Kirkland, WA

LANDSCAPE TECHNICIAN BOOTCAMP Friday, April 3, 2026 Lake Washington Institute of Technology – 11605 132nd Avenue NE, Kirkland, WA 98034-8506 TITLE SPONSOR - DURANTA The Washington Association of Landscape Professionals is excited to offer the LANDSCAPE TECHNICIAN BOOTCAMP training in partnership with the National Association of Landscape Professionals. WALP will be offering the LANDSCAPE TECHNICIAN BOOTCAMP in APRIL 3, 2026 at Lake Washington Insitute of Technology that will be comprehensive training for your staff and workforce. Hands-On Training For Your Crews! The new Landscape Technician Boot Camp will provide hands-on training for your crews. Based on the curriculum for the Landscape Industry Certified Technician Exterior exam, this industry-vetted training will help prepare your crews to work more effectively in 2026. Boot Camp includes training in techniques and safety in 15 different competency areas organized into 3 disciplines presented in Spanish and English! Bonus! This course also helps prepare people for the Landscape Industry Certified Technician Exterior exam. So, once you’ve trained your team, consider registering them to become certified through the National Association of Landscape Professionals \(NALP\). Registration Costs: WALP Members \(First 5 registrants\): $95.00 per person WALP Members \(6\+ registrants\): $80.00 per person NON-WALP Members : $145.00 per person

Event details

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Friday, April 3, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

Event details

item.title: Always Hope Easter Run 5K/10K/13.1 SEATTLE
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 7:30 AM

Location: Magnuson Park (Course Map will be emailed), 7400 Sand Point Way Northeast, Seattle, WA

Set a Goal and Run in this Smaller Group Race through a beautiful route. Run Stress-free, Warm memories, Always here for you!

Event details

item.title: Journaling the Garden
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Bellevue Botanical Garden, 12001 Main St, Bellevue, WA

Nature journaling at the Bellevue Botanical Garden is a wonderful way to connect with the environment through curiosity and observation of the natural world. This free adult program will help guide your practice whether you are just starting out or are an experienced journaler. In this warm and welcoming environment, you will be encouraged to experiment and find your voice. In each session we will gather for introductions and brief guidance, and then head out into Garden to journal and come back together to share experiences.   Please bring a dedicated journal and something to write with. We will meet at 9am the first Saturday of January-November 2026. Registration is recommended but not required.  This free adult program is facilitated by Anne Dziok, Adult Education Program Manager. Anne is a practicing multi-media artist who shows her work regionally.    Email us  with questions.

Event details

item.title: Eggstravaganza Spring Festival
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Heatherwood Middle School, 1728 142nd Pl SE, Mill Creek, WA

Join the City of Mill Creek for our Eggstravaganza Spring Festival! The event features a continuous egg hunt, where children ages one to eleven can search for eggs that can be exchanged for candy at multiple locations throughout the event. Enjoy photos with the Easter Bunny, family friendly activities and games! Join us for the FREE community event at the Heatherwood Middle School track \(1419 Trillium Blvd. SE, Mill Creek\). Parking is limited, so we encourage you to walk, bike, or carpool.

Event details

item.title: Waystone Warhammer 40,000 RTT
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Waystone Wargaming Speakeasy, 1736 NW Market St, Seattle, WA

A ten-person organized play event for Warhammer 40,000 using the current Grand Tournament mission rules with awards and prizes! Sign up here. Members receive 48-hour priority access as well as 20% off admission at checkout. What is an RTT? RTT stands for "Rogue Trader Tournament". This is just another way of saying "small tournament" \(as opposed to a Grand Tournament, or GT\). They're great for simply getting in a full day of games, practicing for larger events, or to experiment with new army compositions.

Event details

item.title: Volunteer Work Party at Seattle's Largest Urban Farm
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 10 AM

Event Types: Volunteer/Work Party Neighborhoods: Rainier Beach/Othello Sponsoring Organization: Green Seattle Partnership Contact: Tilth Alliance Contact Phone: 206-633-0451 x108 Contact Email: cameryntam@tilthalliance.org Audience: All Pre-Register: No Link: http://seattle.greencitypartnerships.org/event/43370/ Join us for a restoration work party at Rainier Beach Urban Farm and Wetland - See website for important details on what to bring and where to meet.

Event details

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

Event details

item.title: Earth Month Event | Restoration at Gallagher Hill Open Space
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 10 AM

Celebrate Earth Month through Volunteering in your Local Park! Gallagher Hill Open Space is a beautiful park with old trees, a thriving stream, and plenty of native plant and animal life. A lot of the park is unfortunately being overgrown with blackberry and ivy, threating the health of this vital ecosystem. Situated above the freeway Gallagher Hil l is an important respite of nature that is well worth protecting. Come jo in us for a park clean-up where we will be removing ivy. It will be a grea t chance to get your hands in the dirt, learn about native plants and our local forests, and work alongside neighbors and friends! No experience necessary and all ages welcome; gloves and tools are provided. Please note: we require participants aged 14 and under to volunteer with a pare nt or guardian at a ratio of 1 adult to 3 kids. When: Saturday, April 4th, 10am-1pm Where: Gallagher Hill Open Space off of Shorewood Dr, follow VOLUNTEER EVENT Signs What to bring: Dress for the weather in cloth es you don't mind getting dirty \(think: long pants, boots or sneakers\). Bring a water bottle. Small snacks, gloves, and tools will be provided. Registration: https://volunteersignup.org/KMTKY

Event details

item.title: Sailing Shore School
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: CWB Boathouse, Lake Union, Seattle, WA

This 2-hour interactive course will introduce you to the basics of sailing. Topics will include terminology for parts of the boat and points of sail, as well as the basic theory of sailing. With the class you will receive the CWB Sailing Primer! Shore School can be taken independently or as part of the SailNOW! or US Sailing Basic Keelboat Certification courses. More information here

Event details

item.title: 3XBA Spring 3x3 Clinic Series
item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: The Bush School, 3400 E Harrison St, Seattle, WA

Neighborhoods: Capitol Hill,Central Area/Squire Park,First Hill,Judkins Park,Madison Park,Madrona/Leschi,Montlake/Portage BayEvent Types: Community,Education,Hobbies,Holiday/Seasonal Events, Sports/Recreation,Youth Audience: Children,Special Needs,Teen S ponsoring Organization: 3x3 Basketball Association \(3XBA\) Contact: Lakin Roland Contact Email: info@3xba.com Pre-Register: Yes Cost: $250 \+ fees Registration Details: Two sessions offered each Sunday: Grades 2-5 from 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM, Grades 6-8 from 11:00 AM to 1:00 PM. The clinic runs for five Sundays: March 22, March 29, April 5, Apr il 26, and May 3, 2026 at The Bush School, Seattle. Early bird pricin g ends on March 1. Learn more about 3xBA and register at: https://www.3xba.com/register Link: https://3xba.ludus.com/index.php?show\_id=200517791 Help your athlete grow their game this spring with the 3xBA Spring 3x3 Clinic Series-a 5-week program combining fun, competit ive 3x3 basketball with small-group instruction led by 3xBA coaches. Players develop skills, confidence, and basketball IQ through structured play and hands-on coaching. Open to all experience levels for 2nd-8th graders. Athletes should bring a basketball and water bottle. Perfe ct for off-season development and team-style competition.

Event details

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. item.title: Meditation, Breathwork, Cold 3rd Sunday of the month at Origins Unity
item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 11:30 AM

Location: Origins Unity LLC, 229 NW 56th Street, Seattle, WA

Meditation, Breathwork, Cold Plunge & Community! Guided session w/ Certified Yoga, QiGong,Trauma Sensitive, Meditation & Breathwork Teacher

Event details

item.title: Spy Masters Academy
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: Suncatcher Forest School, 18701 se 60th st, Issaquah, WA

Class Experience Calling all young secret agents! This time kids are welcome to join action-packed Outdoor Spy Camp, where kids will train to become top-notch spies through thrilling challenges, daring missions, and teamwork! Obstacle Courses & Ninja Training – Agents-in-training will test their agility, speed, and stealth as they navigate exciting ninja courses, crawl through the forest and learn to use nature objects to complete the challenge. Scavenger Hunts & Riddle Solving – Spies must sharpen their detective skills by decoding secret messages, solving riddles, and uncovering hidden clues to complete their top-secret missions. Nerf Battles & Tactical Challenges – Gear up for friendly Nerf wars, where teamwork and strategy are key to outsmarting the opposition. Agents will also practice their precision and accuracy with target practice and stealth maneuvers. Escape Room-Style Puzzles – Secret agents will work together to crack codes, unlock treasure chests, and solve mind-bending puzzles before time runs out! Outdoor Adventure & Camouflage Training – Spies will blend into their surroundings, practice silent movement, and use nature to their advantage while completing their missions. This high-energy camp is perfect for kids who love adventure, problem-solving, and action-packed fun in the great outdoors! Are you ready to accept the mission? - follow the ticket link to book the camp!

Event details

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. item.title: Washington Huskies Baseball vs. Gonzaga Bulldogs Baseball
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: Husky Ballpark, Walla Walla Rd, Seattle, WA

Event details

item.title: Skyline Booster Club | Board Meeting
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Skyline High School, 1122 228th Ave SE, Sammamish, WA

Come join the Booster Club meeting in the Skyline Library to get to know other booster parents and see how you can get involved. Not sure what Boosters is or how to support? This is another great way to get involved or find out more. We can't wait to see you!

Event details

item.title: The Garden w/ Ghost Mountain
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Showbox SoDo, 1700 1st Ave. South, Seattle, WA

THE GARDEN IS A CONSTANTLY EVOLVING BAND ORIGINALLY FROM ORANGE COUNTY, CALIFORNIA. ESTABLISHED IN 2011 BY WYATT AND FLETCHER SHEARS. THEY HAVE BEEN TOURING SINCE 2012 AND HAVE PLAYED IN DIFFERENT REGIONS INCLUDING THE UNITED KINGDOM, EUROPE, UNITED STATES, ASIA, AUSTRALIA, MEXICO & CANADA, THEY CREATED THE TERM, "VADA VADA" IN 2011 TO REPRESENT THEIR MUSIC AND OTHER CREATIONS. VADA VADA IS A TERM THAT REPRESENTS TOTAL FREEDOM OF EXPRESSION WITHOUT BOUNDARIES OR GUIDELINES OF ANY SORT. THEY HAVE BECOME KNOWN FOR THEIR ENERGETIC AND OFF THE WALL PERFORMANCES AROUND THE WORLD, AND ARE PROLIFICALLY TOURING, RELEASING MUSIC AND OTHER CONTENT.

Event details

item.title: Earth Month Event | Mindfulness Walk
item.description:
Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 11 AM

Celebrate Earth Month with a Mindfulness Walk in the Park! Taking inspiration from the Japanese idea of shinrin-yoku \(forest bathin g\), we will be hosting a series of mindfulness walks in Pioneer Park. Mindfulness walking in the forest has shown to have a myriad of benefits from improving heart health to reducing stress \(https://www.npr.org/2023/08/22 /1195337204/a-guide-to-forest-bathing\). Forest Steward Marilyn Cornwe ll \(https://www.soulscapes.us/\) will be leading an hour-long guided walk t hrough the park. Please join us if your curious about mindfulness, want to learn more about and experience healing powers of being in nature, and/or if you want to deepen your connection with Pioneer Park and others. When: Tuesday, April 4th, 11am-12pm Where: Pioneer Park NW, Meet at the corner of Island Crest Way and SE 68th St. Follow VOLUNTEER EVENT Signs What to bring: Dress for the weather in clothes and shoes you will comfo rtable walking and standing in, as well as water.

Event details

item.title: Washington Huskies Baseball vs. Gonzaga Bulldogs Baseball
item.description:
Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Husky Ballpark, Walla Walla Rd, Seattle, WA

Event details

item.title: Friends of Lake Sammamish State Park Lakeside Lecture Bats Bats Bats
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: Lake Sammamish State Park, 2010 Northwest Sammamish Road, Issaquah, WA

Friends of Lake Sammamish State Park, welcome Bats Northwest Board member and biologist Bernice Tannebaum for a Lakeside Lecture about Bats of Northwest, bat boxes and luckily for anyone who attends this event, the incredible depth of Bernice bat knowledge. She was featured this summer in the Seattle Times. The event is a free indoor event but a donation gift is incurabed to help continue the great programming of the Friends of Lake Sammamish. https://wl.donorperfect.net/weblink/weblink.aspx?name=E362706&id=103

Event details

item.title: Tai Chi for Health and Balance
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Seattle Central College Continuing Education, 1701 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Class Overview Tai Chi is a health sustaining exercise and a tool for improved body awareness. Practiced by the elderly for balance and circulation and the young for relaxation and martial arts, it is a wonderful holistic activity for all ages. We'll focus on learning the entire Yang short form, and you'll receive guidance in developing your own regular practice at home. During this class, you'll learn: -The Yang Tai Chi short form, which is comprised of 35 individual movements -Chi Kung, a warm-up exercise and relaxation activity with 18 movements -Elements of Chinese Medical Philosophy, such as Yin/Yang theory and Five Elements transformation -the basics of Taoist philosophy Learning Objectives Learn basic warm-up stretches to improve your overall movement Discover the Yang-style short form of Tai Chi Practice the Yang-style under the direction of an expert instructor Gain knowledge about the history and background of Tai Chi Who Might Enjoy This Class? Individuals seeking a low-impact exercise routine Anyone interested in improving their coordination and balance People interested in developing their focus, relaxation and overall wellness

Event details

item.title: Moonlit Hike: See Poo Poo Point from a Different Light
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: High School Trail, Southeast Evans Street, Issaquah, WA

Event details

item.title: Restoration Event | Native Plant Nursery
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 12 PM

First Thursdays in the Native Plant Nursery! Calling all n ative plant lovers! We're looking for volunteers to join us every first Th ursday of each month to help us care for the plants in our native plant nu rsery. Every year we host a couple thousand native plants in the nursery u ntil they're ready to be put in the ground in parks across the island. This will be a great opportunity to learn more about native plants, learn about basic plant care, and help us ensure our plants healthy before being put in their new homes. Tasks may include: weeding potted pla nts, up-potting or re-potting native plants, plant organizing, plant propagation, weeding gravel areas, tool care, nursery pot maintenance, a nd watering. When: Thursdays, 12pm-2pm \(join us on your lunch break!\) Where: Luther Burbank Park, Caretakers building What to bring: Dress for the weather in clothes you don't mind getting dirty \(think: long pants , boots or sneakers\). Bring water. Small snacks, gloves, and tools will be provided. Registration: https://volunteersignup.org/B4AFB

Event details

item.title: Seattle Mariners - Pregame Field Passes
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 3:40 PM

Location: T-Mobile Park, 1516 First Ave. S., Seattle, WA

REQUIRES GAME TICKET. ALL SALES FINAL. The Pregame Field Pass Experience begins at the Muckleshoot Diamond Club and Press Club Entrance, accessible off of Edgar Martinez Drive. Children 3 years old and younger will not require a Pregame Field Pass ticket. For start times and additional information, please visit Mariners.com/PregameFieldPass.

Event details

item.title: PNW Regional Golden Gloves
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: PRELIMS, April 10, 2026 @ Nomad Skyway, 12600 Renton Ave S, WA 98178. Doors Open at 5:00 pm FINALS: April 11, 2026 @ F remont Studios, 155 N 35th St, Seattle, WA

Event Description: Nomad Boxing Club is proud to host the PNW Regional Golden Gloves in Seattle for the first time ever, bringing elite amateur boxers from Washington, Oregon, and Alaska together for a two-day championship event and the official pathway to the National Golden Gloves. Featuring a special appearance by boxing legend Roberto Durán, this high-energy weekend will showcase championship-level competition, sellout crowds, and a historic moment for Nomad and the Seattle box ing community. Neighborhoods: Alki/Admiral,Arbor Heights,Ballard,B eacon Hill,Belltown,Broadview/Bitter Lake,Capitol Hill,Cascade/Eastl ake/South Lake Union,Cedar Park/Meadowbrook,Central Area/Squire Park, Chinatown-International District,Citywide,Columbia City,Delridge/Nort h Delridge,Downtown Commercial Core,Duwamish/SODO,East of Seattle,Fa untleroy/Morgan Junction/Seaview,First Hill,Fremont,Georgetown,Green Lake,Greenwood/Phinney Ridge,Haller Lake,High Point,Highland Park, Interbay,Judkins Park,Lake City/Olympic Hills/Victory Heights,Laurelh urst/Sand Point,Licton Springs,Madison Park,Madrona/Leschi,Magnolia ,Miller Park,Montlake/Portage Bay,Mt. Baker/North Rainier,North Beach /Blue Ridge,North Beacon Hill/Jefferson Park,North Capitol Hill,North of Seattle,Northgate/Maple Leaf,Pioneer Square,Queen Anne,Rainier B each/Othello,Ravenna/Bryant/Roosevelt,Riverview,Roxhill/Westwood/Whit e Center,Seward Park,South Beacon Hill/New Holly,South of Seattle,So uth Park,Sunset Hill/Loyal Heights,University District,Uptown,Wallin gford,Wedgwood/View Ridge,West of Seattle,West Seattle Junction/Genes ee Hill,Whittier Heights DON Programs: Community Happenings Event Types: Community,Special Events,Sports/Recreation Audience: All Sponsoring Organization: Nomad Boxing Club Contact: Manny Dunham Contact Phone: 206-851-7269 Contact Email: nomadboxingclub@gmail.com Site Contact Details: Manny Dunham Pre-Register: Yes Cost: $25-$200 Link: https://nomadboxingclub.com/

Event details

Friday, April 10, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: Logan Field, Seattle, WA

Event details

item.title: Earth Month Event | Restoration at Ellis Pond
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Celebrate Earth Month at our Ellis Pond Work Party Join us at Ellis Pond Park for a Forest-Steward led weeding and clean-up event fo r Earth Month. Removing certain plants will help build even more wildlife habitat in this small neighborhood park teeming with birds and other woodl and creatures. We will remove weeds like English ivy, Himalayan blackberry, and English holly to help protect native plants and forest health. Come join friends and neighbors in this fun Saturday event. No experie nce necessary; gloves and tools are provided. Participation is limited, so please register. Please note: we require participants aged 14 and under to volunteer with a parent or guardian at a ratio of 1 adult to 3 ki ds. When: Saturday, April 11th, 9:30am-11:30pm Where: Ellis Pond Park \(meet at the bench next to the boardwalk on 90th Ave SE\) What to bring: Dress for the weather in clothes you don't mind getting dirty \(think: lon g pants, boots or sneakers\). Bring a water bottle. Small snacks, gloves, and tools will be provided. And a snack or dish to share. Registration : volunteersignup.org/Y8XQX

Event details

item.title: Foraging for Basketry and Wild Crafts
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Bellevue Botanical Garden, 12001 Main St, Bellevue, WA

Taught by basket maker and teacher Erin Cox. Join the conversation about the incredible plants that grow here in the Pacific Northwest and explore ways to identify, gather, store and use them to create baskets and crafts that bring nature inside. Whether you live in an urban, suburban or rural setting, there are forage-able crafting and weaving materials to be found. Daylily, daffodil, dandelion, crocosmia, iris, cattails, juncus, bulrush, ivy, honeysuckle, wisteria, dogwood, willow and more! Erin will share about her approach to sustainable and responsible foraging. We should all care for the plants we harvest and preserve the environments they grow in. This discussion is good for beginners and you will walk away with some fun, easy ways to get started. $17.50 BBGS Member/$25 Non-Member Erin found her life’s passion in basketry and wild crafting. In 2024 she established a small basketry willow farm in Carnation, Washington, where she invites students to learn, harvest, and engage with the materials. In 2025, she opened Field and Forest Crafts and her own private teaching studio in NE Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Family Saturday: Get Ready for Animal MasqueRAVE
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Olympic Sculpture Park, 2901 Western Ave, Seattle, WA

Spend a fun-filled morning with your family learning about art and the environment, and get ready for Animal MasqueRAVE on May 9 with guidance from a teaching artist! This program takes place outside at the Olympic Sculpture Park on the covered north terrace of the PACCAR Pavilion. Walk-ins welcome on a first-come, first-served basis.

Event details

item.title: Saturdays at SAM: WALK DON'T RUN
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Play, create, and connect in the Brotman Forum every first, second, and third Saturday, 11 am–2 pm. This lively space is filled with hands-on art making, live music, and moments of community—all inspired by SAM’s permanent collections and exhibitions. Whether you’re an art enthusiast, a curious newcomer, or just looking for a place to recharge this weekend, you’re always welcome. Learn about the artwork through interactive activities, meet new friends, or simply enjoy the atmosphere before exploring the galleries. Programs in SAM’s gathering spaces in the museum’s entry area are always free and open to everyone. Bring your curiosity and imagination, we’ll provide the rest.

Event details

item.title: 3XBA Spring 3x3 Clinic Series
item.description:
Sunday, April 12, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: The Bush School, 3400 E Harrison St, Seattle, WA

Neighborhoods: Capitol Hill,Central Area/Squire Park,First Hill,Judkins Park,Madison Park,Madrona/Leschi,Montlake/Portage BayEvent Types: Community,Education,Hobbies,Holiday/Seasonal Events, Sports/Recreation,Youth Audience: Children,Special Needs,Teen S ponsoring Organization: 3x3 Basketball Association \(3XBA\) Contact: Lakin Roland Contact Email: info@3xba.com Pre-Register: Yes Cost: $250 \+ fees Registration Details: Two sessions offered each Sunday: Grades 2-5 from 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM, Grades 6-8 from 11:00 AM to 1:00 PM. The clinic runs for five Sundays: March 22, March 29, April 5, Apr il 26, and May 3, 2026 at The Bush School, Seattle. Early bird pricin g ends on March 1. Learn more about 3xBA and register at: https://www.3xba.com/register Link: https://3xba.ludus.com/index.php?show\_id=200517791 Help your athlete grow their game this spring with the 3xBA Spring 3x3 Clinic Series-a 5-week program combining fun, competit ive 3x3 basketball with small-group instruction led by 3xBA coaches. Players develop skills, confidence, and basketball IQ through structured play and hands-on coaching. Open to all experience levels for 2nd-8th graders. Athletes should bring a basketball and water bottle. Perfe ct for off-season development and team-style competition.

Event details

item.title: Spy Maters Camp II
item.description:
Monday, April 13, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: Suncatcher Forest School, 18701 se 60th st, Issaquah, WA

Calling all young secret agents! This time kids are welcome to join action-packed Outdoor Spy Camp, where kids will train to become top-notch spies through thrilling challenges, daring missions, and teamwork! Obstacle Courses & Ninja Training – Agents-in-training will test their agility, speed, and stealth as they navigate exciting ninja courses, crawl through the forest and learn to use nature objects to complete the challenge. Scavenger Hunts & Riddle Solving – Spies must sharpen their detective skills by decoding secret messages, solving riddles, and uncovering hidden clues to complete their top-secret missions. Nerf Battles & Tactical Challenges – Gear up for friendly Nerf wars, where teamwork and strategy are key to outsmarting the opposition. Agents will also practice their precision and accuracy with target practice and stealth maneuvers. Escape Room-Style Puzzles – Secret agents will work together to crack codes, unlock treasure chests, and solve mind-bending puzzles before time runs out! Outdoor Adventure & Camouflage Training – Spies will blend into their surroundings, practice silent movement, and use nature to their advantage while completing their missions. This high-energy camp is perfect for kids who love adventure, problem-solving, and action-packed fun in the great outdoors! Are you ready to accept the mission? - follow the link to book a spot!

item.title: Global Soccer Game Webinar
item.description:
Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 2:30 PM

Location: Virtually via zoom, This event will be recorded and distributed after the event to all those who register., Tukwila, WA

Join Us for the FIFA Ready Webinar Date: February 10th, 2:30 PM Location: Virtually Via Zoom Event Overview: As we gear up for the monumental FIFA events, it&'s crucial to align our regional efforts to maximize economic and infrastructural benefits. This meeting will gather key stakeholders from various sectors, including our cities and business community, to strategize and synchronize our readiness plans with an emphasis in preparing our business community to ensure you are ready for this economic opportunity. So do you want to learn about what resources are available to you to maximize this time? Register to learn more now. These quarterly events are brought to you by the Seattle Southside Chamber of Commerce and Explore Seattle Southside. This meeting is a pivotal opportunity for stakeholders to collaborate and ensure our region is fully prepared to host FIFA events successfully. Together, we can create a unified and effective execution plan that benefits all.

Event details

item.title: Oklou
item.description:
Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Showbox SoDo, 1700 1st Ave. South, Seattle, WA

French vocalist, producer and composer Oklou returned with "family and friends" in September 2024, the first solo single since her highly celebrated full-length mixtape galore. The new song is an ethereal, haunting reflection on the mystery of life, with production that features arpeggiated marimbas, lush choral textures and intricate sound design. "Are you even real?" she asks in her cryptic lyrics. The single arrives alongside a music video by director Gil Gharbi that shows Oklou and her friends running through desolate suburban streets as the camera darts searchingly. Describing the track, Oklou shares, "'family and friends' feels like a battle almost. where I'd be searching for answers to unstated questions, escaping from something intangible, invisible. A memory? Maybe I like the idea that I'd be running away from something, and I'm looking for good reasons to run. Maybe I just like that." Born Marylou Mayniel in the countryside of Western France, Oklou's first musical experiences consisted of classical piano and cello lessons before she fell in love with electronic music and took up production. Mayniel eventually moved to Paris, where she hosted weekly radio shows and performed her first live shows as Oklou. After two EPs, 2017's For The Beasts and 2018's The Rite Of May, Mayniel received international acclaim, accelerated through collaborations with Mura Masa, A. G. Cook and EASYFUN \(the last two both producers of Charli XCX's Brat\) and culminating in her full-length mixtape galore in 2020. It was a turning point in her artistic life, heralded as an innovative and introspective work by fans and media, with Dazed praising as "sophisticated in its composition yet totally cutting edge in its sound." International touring followed, including support tours with Oneohtrix Point Never and Caroline Polachek.

Event details
